qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ZIP path traversal (ZipSlip) in safe_extract allows arbitrary file write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (safe_extract in guarddog/utils/archives.py) but different root cause and vulnerability class. The advisory describes a zip bomb vulnerability (CWE-409) due to missing size validation for decompressed files causing DoS. This finding describes path traversal (CWE-22) due to missing validation of '../' sequences in member filenames causing arbitrary file write. These are fundamentally different security flaws in the same function.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described zip bomb vulnerability (CWE-409). The advisory describes missing size validation for decompressed ZIP files in guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract() causing denial of service through disk/resource exhaustion. The qualifying finding describes path traversal (CWE-22) in the same safe_extract() function but with a different root cause (missing '../' validation vs missing size/compression validation) and different impact (arbitrary file write vs DoS). While the path traversal finding affects the same function as the advisory, it does not identify the same vulnerable code/root cause mechanism.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-ffj4-jq7m-9g6v describes CWE-409: Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Zip Bomb) with missing decompressed size limits, compression ratio validation, file count limits, and total extracted size validation in guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract().

Finding 'zip-path-traversal-safe-extract' describes CWE-22: Path Traversal (ZipSlip) in guarddog/utils/archives.py safe_extract() with missing '../' validation in member filenames before calling zip_file.extract().

The advisory root cause (missing size/compression validation) is distinct from the path traversal root cause (missing path sanitization), representing two independent security flaws in the same function.
